setup.title: Development Environment Setup
setup.content:
To set up your development environment:

1. **Install Prerequisites**: Ensure you have Git (2.40+), Python (3.11+), and Docker (24+) installed.
2. **Clone the repo**: `git clone https://github.com/company/platform.git`
3. **Install dependencies**: Run `pip install -r requirements.txt` in the backend folder, or `npm install` in the frontend folder.
4. **Configure environment variables**: Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and fill in the secrets provided by your team lead.
5. **Start services**: Run `docker-compose up -d` to spin up all dependency services (PostgreSQL, Redis, etc.).
6. **Verify setup**: Run `make verify` to check that all services are running correctly.

If you run into issues, check the `#dev-setup` Slack channel or the Troubleshooting Guide in Confluence.

repository.title: Repository Access & Cloning
repository.content:
**Repository Structure:**

- `platform-backend` — Python/FastAPI microservices (main API)
- `platform-frontend` — React/TypeScript customer-facing app
- `infra` — Terraform + Helm configs for Kubernetes clusters
- `data-pipelines` — Spark/Airflow ETL pipelines
- `ml-models` — ML training and serving code

**How to clone:**
```bash
git clone git@github.com:company/platform-backend.git
git clone git@github.com:company/platform-frontend.git
```

**Access**: Request GitHub access via the IT portal (it.company.com). Your manager needs to approve before you gain access. SSH keys must be registered in your GitHub profile.

architecture.title: System Architecture Overview
architecture.content:
**Platform Architecture:**

We use a **microservices architecture** deployed on **Kubernetes (EKS)**:

```
[Client Apps] → [API Gateway (Kong)] → [Backend Services]
                                           ├── Auth Service (Python/FastAPI)
                                           ├── User Service (Python/FastAPI)
                                           ├── Payment Service (Java/Spring)
                                           ├── Notification Service (Node.js)
                                           └── Analytics Service (Python)
                                      [Data Layer]
                                           ├── PostgreSQL (primary DB)
                                           ├── Redis (caching + queues)
                                           ├── Elasticsearch (search)
                                           └── S3 (object storage)
```

**Key principles**: Event-driven (Kafka for async), REST + gRPC for inter-service comms, 12-factor app methodology.

backend.title: Running Backend Services
backend.content:
**Running the backend locally:**

```bash
# 1. Navigate to backend directory
cd platform-backend

# 2. Create and activate virtual environment
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate

# 3. Install dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt

# 4. Start with Docker Compose (recommended)
docker-compose up

# OR start individual service
uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
```

The API will be available at `http://localhost:8000`. API docs (Swagger) at `http://localhost:8000/docs`.

**Running tests**: `pytest tests/ -v --cov=app`

frontend.title: Running Frontend Application
frontend.content:
**Running the frontend locally:**

```bash
# 1. Navigate to frontend directory
cd platform-frontend

# 2. Install Node.js dependencies
npm install

# 3. Set up environment
cp .env.example .env.local
# Edit .env.local with your local API URL

# 4. Start development server
npm run dev
```

The app will be available at `http://localhost:3000`.

**Build for production**: `npm run build`
**Run tests**: `npm test`
**Lint code**: `npm run lint`

We use React 18, TypeScript, Vite, TailwindCSS, and React Query.

coding_standards.title: Coding Standards & Style Guide
coding_standards.content:
**Python (Backend):**
- Follow PEP 8; use `black` for formatting, `ruff` for linting
- Type hints required on all public functions
- Docstrings required for all modules and public classes
- Max line length: 88 chars
- Run `make lint` before committing

**TypeScript (Frontend):**
- ESLint + Prettier (run `npm run lint` and `npm run format`)
- Functional components only; use React hooks
- Props must be typed with TypeScript interfaces
- No `any` types — use proper generics

**General:**
- Commit messages: Conventional Commits format (`feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`)
- Branch naming: `feature/<ticket-id>-short-description`
- PRs require 2 approvals + passing CI
- All secrets via environment variables — never hardcode
- Write tests for all new features (aim for 80%+ coverage)

security.title: Security Policies
security.content:
**Security Requirements:**

1. **Secrets Management**: All secrets stored in AWS Secrets Manager or HashiCorp Vault. Never commit secrets to git.
2. **Authentication**: We use OAuth 2.0 + JWT tokens (15-min access tokens, 7-day refresh tokens).
3. **Authorization**: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC). Request minimum permissions needed.
4. **Data Handling**: PII must be encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3).
5. **Dependency Security**: Run `pip audit` and `npm audit` regularly. Critical CVEs must be patched within 24 hours.
6. **Code Scanning**: All PRs run SAST via Snyk and CodeQL.
7. **Incident Response**: Any suspected breach → immediately alert `security@company.com` and `#security-incidents` Slack.

**Compliance**: We are SOC 2 Type II and GDPR compliant. Complete mandatory security training within your first week.

devops.title: DevOps & Deployment Guide
devops.content:
**CI/CD Pipeline:**
- GitHub Actions for CI; ArgoCD for CD
- Every PR triggers: lint → test → build → security scan
- Merging to `main` auto-deploys to **staging** environment
- Production deploys require a release PR + on-call engineer approval

**Infrastructure:**
- Cloud: AWS (EKS, RDS, ElastiCache, S3, CloudFront)
- IaC: Terraform (in `infra/` repo)
- Container orchestration: Kubernetes with Helm charts
- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty for alerts

**Deployment commands (staging):**
```bash
make deploy-staging SERVICE=auth-service
```

**Accessing environments:**
- `kubectl config use-context staging-cluster`
- `kubectl get pods -n platform`

onboarding_process.title: Onboarding Process
onboarding_process.content:
**Welcome to the team! 🎉**

**Week 1:**
- Complete company security training (mandatory)
- Set up development environment
- Read architecture documentation
- Shadow a senior developer
- Fix your first 'good-first-issue' ticket

**Week 2:**
- Join your first sprint planning
- Pair program on a small feature
- Complete your first code review
- Set up 1:1 with your manager

**Week 3-4:**
- Take ownership of a small feature
- Participate in team demos
- Complete all onboarding checklists

**Resources:**
- Engineering Wiki (Notion): notion.company.com
- Slack: #engineering, #your-team, #dev-setup, #general
- Jira for project tracking
- Figma for design specs

testing.title: Testing Guidelines
testing.content:
**Testing Strategy:**

We follow the testing pyramid:
- **Unit Tests** (70%): Fast, isolated, mock external deps
- **Integration Tests** (20%): Test component interactions
- **E2E Tests** (10%): Critical user journeys only

**Python:**
```bash
pytest tests/ -v                    # Run all tests
pytest tests/unit/ -v               # Unit tests only
pytest --cov=app --cov-report=html  # With coverage
```

**TypeScript:**
```bash
npm test                  # Run all tests
npm run test:coverage     # With coverage report
npm run test:e2e          # Playwright E2E tests
```

**CI Gate**: All tests must pass + coverage must be ≥ 80% for PRs to merge.
